system.description: Rogue physician who carries field medicine, surgery, and humanitarian grit into dangerous places.
system.notes: Play the character as a healer first, but keep a weapon close when the wounded must be protected.

grantedAbilities.name: Field Doctor
grantedAbilities.notes: Use the class as the party surgeon, diagnostician, and emergency healer whenever lives are on the line.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Hand to Hand counts as an other skill. Basic costs one selection, Expert costs two, Martial Arts costs three, and Assassin costs four.

unresolvedNotes: Roll starting money from the city doctor or wandering fixer option in the class text.
When working on bionics, the character suffers the listed penalty from the class description. Alien bodies and alien cybernetics are also harder to treat.
At least two related skills must be chosen from Technical or Science.

system.description: Streetwise down-sider who survives through speed, attitude, black-market savvy, and urban nerve.
system.notes: Lean into alleys, tunnels, rooftops, and the lower city whenever the character needs an edge.

grantedAbilities.name: Street Survivor
grantedAbilities.notes: Use sewers, service tunnels, rooftops, and black-market contacts to move, hide, and get what the crew needs.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Start with Hand to Hand: Basic. The character may upgrade to Martial Arts, or Assassin if evil, by spending one related skill.

unresolvedNotes: Check the role-specific attribute suggestions from the class text if the character is built as a hacker, thief, assassin, or thug.
Choose 1D4+1 starting cybernetics manually from the allowed black-market and sensory categories.
At least three related skills must come from Physical and two from Rogue.

system.description: Cybernetic surgeon and implant specialist who keeps clinics, chop-shops, and frontier medicine running.
system.notes: Play the character as a healer who understands steel and flesh equally well, even when the work must be done off the books.

occSkills.name: W.P. Knife
occSkills.notes: Gain +1 to strike when fighting with a knife.

grantedAbilities.name: Cybernetic Surgery
grantedAbilities.notes: Use the class to install, remove, repair, and modify cybernetics when the right tools and facilities are available.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Hand to Hand counts as an other skill. Basic costs one selection, Expert costs two, Martial Arts costs three, and Assassin costs four.

unresolvedNotes: If the character has implants, choose 1D4+2 starting cybernetics manually from the allowed categories.
W.P. Knife grants its extra strike bonus only when the character is actually fighting with a knife.
At least two related skills must be chosen from Technical.

system.description: Mercenary mechanic and engineer who can keep old-world machines and modern war gear alive.
system.notes: Treat the character as the crew?s repair bench, jury-rig expert, and machine whisperer.

grantedAbilities.name: Machine Compatibility
grantedAbilities.notes: The character feels at home around machinery and takes only half the normal penalty when dealing with alien machines or techno-wizard devices.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Start with Hand to Hand: Basic. Upgrade to Expert by spending one other skill, or to Martial Arts or Assassin by spending two.

unresolvedNotes: At character creation, roll percentile dice. On 1-40 the Operator is a minor psionic. If psychic, roll the starting I.S.P. and choose three powers from the class list or the single super ability of telemechanics.
At least two related skills must be chosen from Mechanical.
The class can begin with nearly any non-magical vehicle, but the exact model still needs to be chosen manually.

system.description: Artifact hunter, teacher, and wanderer who survives by curiosity, memory, and adaptable learning.
system.notes: Play the character as someone who values books, languages, relics, and hard-won knowledge enough to risk the wasteland for them.

grantedAbilities.name: Keeper of Lost Knowledge
grantedAbilities.notes: Use the class to interpret ruins, preserve relics, and connect scraps of old-world information into something useful at the table.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Hand to Hand counts as an other skill. Basic costs one selection, Expert costs two, Martial Arts costs three, and Assassin costs four.

unresolvedNotes: Pick two additional languages manually when the character is created.
At least four related skills must be chosen from Technical and two from Science.
Roll starting money and saleable artifacts from the class text.
